  • Examples

Tile Architecture Headers

Detailed Description

Chip-specific routines that can be used at any layer of the system software stack.

The TILE Architecture™ platform support headers provide access to various low level aspects of the TILE Architecture. These header files can be used by any layer of the software stack (including user space, Linux, the hypervisor, or the bare metal environment), although some hardware resources might not be accessible to code running at a privilege level that is too low.

This guide provides a limited overview of the functionality provided by these headers; more extensive documentation is available in the headers themselves.

This guide provides detailed documentation for the Atomic Instruction Macros (arch/atomic.h), Low-Level UDN Support (arch/udn.h), Cycle Counters (arch/cycle.h), and Simulator Control (arch/sim.h) modules.

The arch/ hierarchy includes headers for a variety of other tasks, including:

  • arch/abi.h : The Tile Processor Application Binary Interface.

  • arch/spr.h : A listing of the various Special Purpose Registers (SPRs) and register field defined by the Tile architecture. For additional information about the SPRs, refer to the Tile Processor System Architecture Manual.

  • Many other headers related to on-chip I/O device registers and packets.


Modules

 Atomic Instruction Macros (arch/atomic.h)
 Atomic instruction macros.
 Low-Level UDN Support (arch/udn.h)
 Low level support for the UDN (user dynamic network).
 Cycle Counters (arch/cycle.h)
 Interface to the "cycle count", which is a 64-bit number, initialized to zero at boot, and incremented at the end of each machine cycle.
 Simulator Control (arch/sim.h)
 An API for controlling the simulator at runtime.
